title: The One With Rachel's New Dress

lines.text: Central Perk, Joey and Chandler are there as Phoebe enters carrying a drum.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Wow, it is true what they say, pregnant bellies look like a drum.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (not amused) Ha-ha. (She sits down on the couch.) No, it's just I'm so pregnant that I-my guitar doesn't fit anymore. So I thought 'til I'm not, I'm just gonna play all my songs on this drum. It sounds really cool!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Listen. Listen. (She starts to play and sing.) Smelly cat, smelly cat, what are they feeding you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow, Pheebs! That sounds great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know! I know, and I've only been playing for like an hour!

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: (entering) Phoebe! Phoebe! Hi! Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Umm, actually, I came down to ask you a big favour.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well, don't tell me you want to keep more of your stuff in my uterus.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: (laughs) No. No. No. (Sits down.) Okay, now, see, I wanna name the girl baby Leslie, and Frank wants to name one of the boy babies Frank JR. JR.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wouldn't that be Frank the III?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Don't get me started. (To Phoebe) Anyway, umm, since there are three babies and umm, we both got to put our names in, we would be truly honoured if you named the other boy baby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! That's so great! Oh! Oh! Cougar.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: You think about it. (Leaves)
Opening Credits
Ross's apartment, he and Emily are getting ready to go to the airport.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I left a bra drying on the shower rod, you don't think your son will think it's yours and be horribly traumatised?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, if mommy can have a wife, daddy can have a bra.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: (checks the clock) Ohh, it's time to go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no-no-no, see, that-that clock's a little fast, uh, we have 17 minutes. Huh, what can we do in 17 minutes? Twice?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Well that's ambitious.
They kiss but are interrupted by a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, uh, you can ignore that.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: That's Carol with your son!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhh, believe me when he's older, he'll understand.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (knocking on the door) Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'll be right there. (He goes over and opens the door to Carol, Susan, and Ben.) (To Ben.) Hello! (To Carol.) Hello! (To Susan.) Hey. Uhh, Emily, this is Carol and Susan.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Hey, it's so nice to finally meet you!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Me too!

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Ohh, y'know, Susan's gonna be shooting a commercial in London next week.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Oh yeah, I'm so excited, I've never been there.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh, well, I'll show you around.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: That would be great! Also, uh, I was hoping to catch a show so if you can make any suggestions...

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh, there's tonnes of terrific stuff-I'll go with you!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Ahh!
Ross accidentally, on purpose, bumps into Susan.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look at you two, bonding, making us late for the airport so...

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Are you all right?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Oh, he's fine. He's fine. It's just that us getting along is difficult for him, because he doesn't like me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh come on! That's-that's... true.
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Chandler are playing foosball as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Do we have a baby name yet?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh! No! This is so hard! I went through this whole book (Holds up a book) and found nothing! I want a name that's really like, y'know strong and confident, y'know? Like-like Exxon.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it certainly worked for that Valdez kid.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh-ooh, Pheebs, you want a strong name? How about, The Hulk?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I'm-I'm not sure about Hulk, but I like the idea of a name starting with "The."

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, want a good name, go with Joey. Joey's your pal. Joey's your buddy. "Where is everybody?" "Well, they're hanging out with Joey."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, y'know what, if you're gonna do that, if you're gonna name him Joey, you should name him Chandler. (Phoebe doesn't think so.) Oh, come on! Chandler's funny, sophisticated, and he's very loveable, once you get to know him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, hey, Joey's loveable too! But the thing about Joey is, if you need him, he'll be there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, Chandler will be there for you too. I mean, well, he might be a little late, but-but, he'll be there. And he'll bring you some cold soda, if want you need him for is that you're really hot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What do ya say? What do ya say?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I, I like the idea of naming him after someone I love, and Joey and Chandler are great names. (They both stare at her.) But, all right, I don't-maybe I'll just name him The Hulk.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I knew I shouldn't have mentioned it! That's what I wanted to name my kid!
Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel's, Monica is cooking and Rachel is getting ready for a date with Joshua.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, Mon, if you were hoping to sleep with Joshua the first time tonight, which one of these would you want to be wearing. (She's holding two frilly, lace nighties.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? It really creeps me out choosing other people's sex clothes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sorry. I'm so exited! I've been waiting for this for months! I got my hair coloured! I got new sheets! I'm making him a very fancy meal.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What am I making him by the way?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you're making him a frieze salad with goat cheese and pine nuts, wild nuts, wild rice, roast asparagus, and salmon au croup.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I thought I was making him filet mignon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, you were, but you decided to make salmon because you had some left over at the restaurant. And then you realised if you (Points at Rachel) bitched about it, then you (Points to herself) would stop cooking, and you (Points at Rachel) would have to make your famous baked potato and Diet Coke.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow, I really get crabby when I cook.
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Joey, Chandler, and Phoebe are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So uh, Emily called last night...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And now you're giving me the message!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Turns out them Emily is just crazy about Susan. Yeah, they're going to the theatre together! They're going to dinner! They're going horseback riding!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God, Susan is so fun!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, this is just a little too familiar, okay? For like, for like six months before Carol and I spilt up, all I heard was: "My friend Susan is so smart. My friend Susan is so funny. My friend Susan is so great."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You actually think that something can happen between Emily and Susan?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, they're going to the gym together! Two women! Stretching! Y'know they-they take a steam together! Things get a little playful-didn't you see Personal Best?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, but I'm gonna!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi! Hi! You're crazy! Okay? This is Emily. Emily is straight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How do you know? I mean we thought Carol was straight before I married her!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I definitely. I don't like the name Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What a weird way to kick me when I'm down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No! I-I meant for the baby!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. What's wrong with Ross?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it's just y'know that something like this would never to like The Hulk, y'know...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Actually that-that's not true, in The Incredible Hulk uh, No. 72, Dr. Bruce Banner found... (Sees everyone staring at him and stops.) Y'know, ugh, nevermind, my girlfriend's a lesbian. (Leaves.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, I decided I'm definitely going to go with either Joey or Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Oh-oh, you gotta pick Joey! I mean, name one famous person named Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Raymond Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Someone you didn't make up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, there are no famous Joey's. Except for, huh, Joey Buttafucco.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, that guy really hurt us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, how about a compromise then, okay? What if it's like y'know, Chanoey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, look, Joey! Come on, think about it, first of all, he'll never be President. There's never gonna be a President Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right look man, I didn't want to bring this up, but Chandler, is the stupidest name I ever heard in my life! It's not even a name; it's barely even a word. Okay? It's kinda like chandelier, but it's not! All right? It's a stupid, stupid non-name!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, you're, you're right. I have a horrible, horrible name.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sorry man, I didn't-I'm-I'm sorry. I'm sorry. (Goes over and comforts him.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So I guess it's Joey then!
Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is on her dinner date with Joshua.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: This is so nice. Thank you for doing this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, please! Cooking soothes me. (They kiss.) Ahh. So, dig in!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Great! Oh, it all looks sooo good!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (taking a bite) Hmmm!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh I know, my God, this is so-this rice is so-I am so good.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Behind you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sees the chick and the duck) Oh, yeah, I'm sorry. They used to live here; sometimes they migrate back over.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: (getting up and backing away from they.) Is there ah, is there some way they can not be here. It's just ah, farm birds really kinda freak me out!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, sure, okay. Okay.
Rachel gets up and ushers them into the hall, as they pass Joshua, he leaps onto the counter to avoid them. Rachel drops them off in the hall, and knocks on Joey's door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (answering the door.) Hey, how did you do that?! Come on in. (He brings them inside.)
[Cut back to Rachel's date.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All gone! So, farm birds, huh?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, it's-it's my only weird thing, I swear. And I-I-I would've told you about it, but I didn't know they would be here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: So, all right.
They both sit back down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. So, can I serve you a little of-What? What? What? (She sees that Joshua isn't relaxed.)

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Nothing I uh, it's just that I know that they're still out there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But, they're across the hall! I mean that's two doors away, it would take them a long time to peck their way back over here.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Okay, that's-that's not funny. Uhh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y'know, would you feel better if we went someplace else? I mean we could pack all this stuff up and y'know go to your apartment.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Oh, they're working on this week, it's a total mess. But uh, I'm staying at my parents' house, we could go there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Your parents'?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, they're out of town.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, it's this huge place, and-and it's got this gorgeous view of the park, and very, very romantic. What do you say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah that works.
He moves to kiss her, but stops when he hears the duck.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: They-they-they can smell fear.
Ross's apartment, Carol has come to pick up Ben.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (opening the door.) Hey!

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Hey! How's Ben?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I asked him if he wanted to eat, he said, "No." I asked him if he wanted to sleep, he said, "No." I asked him what he wanted to do, he said, "No." So, he's sweeping. (We see Ben playing with a broom and a dustpan.)

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Hey, Ben! Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So umm, any word from Susan?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Ooh, yeah! She said she's having sooo much fun with Emily.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh. Uh-huh. Uh, by the by, did it uh, did it ever occur to you that, I don't know, maybe they might be having a little too much fun?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: What's too much fun?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, the kind of fun, you and Susan had when we were married.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Oh my God, you are so paranoid!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Am I?!

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Am I?!

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: I can't speak for Emily, but Susan is in a loving, committed relationship.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh, Carol, so were we. All right, just-just imagine for a moment, Susan meets someone and-and they really hit it off. Y'know? Say-say they're coming back from the theatre, and they-they stop at a pub for a couple of drinks, they're laughing, y'know, someone innocently touches someone else... There's electricity, it's new. It's exciting. Are you telling me there isn't even the slightest possibility of something happening?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Maybe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OH MY GOD!! I didn't really believe it until you just said it!!
Joshua's parents' apartment, Rachel and Joshua are entering.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: ...and even though none of the other kids believed me, I swear to God, that duck pushed me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! This place is fabulous!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, let me show you around. This is the uh, downstairs living room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa-whoa, there's two living rooms? God, growing up here, this place must've been a real babe magnet.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, well, it would've been, but uh, my parents just moved here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, you should know, this place is a real babe magnet. Wanna make out?
They kiss.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Hey, here's an idea. Why don't uh, I put the food in the fridge and we can eat it later?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That sounds like a plan. Umm, is there a place I can go freshen up?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Oh yeah, yeah uh, it's down the hall and uh, second door to your left.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah.
She goes down the hall. Joshua goes to put the food away when his parents walk in.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Burgin
lines.text: Oh, hi, darling!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Mom, Dad, what are you guys doing here?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Burgin
lines.text: Oh, well we cut the trip short.

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: France sucks!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Umm, this may be a little weird, but I-I-I got a date here.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Burgin
lines.text: Oh, say no more!

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: We'll just grab some food and take it with us right upstairs, and we'll be right out of you hair.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Oh, that-that would be great. So you didn't even get to Italy?

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: Yep, sucks!

They all go into the kitchen. Just then, Rachel comes back from the bathroom; she had removed her dress and is wearing nothing but a lace nightie. She tries to find someplace seductive to wait for Joshua. She tries to sit on the piano, but it makes too much noise. So she goes over to the couch and kinda half lays down to wait for Joshua. Joshua comes in from the kitchen, sees Rachel, and freezes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi you!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I can do more than cook.
Just then, his parents enter. Rachel gasps.

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: I like her. She sees smart.
Commercial Break
Joshua's parents' apartment, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Uhh, Rachel, my parents...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! It's so nice to meet you. (She goes over and shakes their hands.) Hello.

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Burgin
lines.text: Hello. Well, Joshua, that $500 was for groceries.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? This-this, no, oh no, no-no-no, this is not-that's-that's not what it is. See, see, okay, I work in fashion, see and-and, this is a real dress actually. It's-it's, they're-they're wearing it in Milan, so part of my job is too wear the clothes, and then I see how people respond, and then I report back to my superiors at Bloomingdale's, so... And obviously in uh, in-in this case, (She grabs a pen and paper) I am going to report back, "USA not ready."

lines.speaker: Mrs. Burgin
lines.text: Maybe in L.A?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: So, have you kids eaten yet?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, we were going to do that after-I mean umm, next.

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: Well, we're starving, why don't we all go get something to eat?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah, well... Yeah, no use wasting this baby, just lyin' around the house.

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: So... We go eat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Mr. Burgin
lines.text: You'll wear that. We'll be eating, and of course, you'll be wearing that.
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is looking for a new name in Phoebe's book of names.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, I am sorry about what I said!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, you're right, it is a ridiculous name!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's not that bad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes it is! From now on, I have no first name.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, you're just Bing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I have no name.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, so, what are we supposed to call you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay uh, for now, temporarily, you can call me, Clint.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No way are you cool enough to pull of Clint.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so what name am I cool enough to pull off?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, Gene.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's Clint. It's Clint! (He heads for his bedroom.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See you later, Gene.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bye, Gene.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's Clint! Clint!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's up with Gene?
Central Perk, Rachel is telling Phoebe and Monica of her date.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, you wore your nightie to dinner?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah. And uh, the best part though, when the uh, waiter spilled water down my back, I jumped up, and my boob popped out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, no!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's all right. I got nice boobs. (Phoebe and Monica nod there heads in agreement.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (returning from the phone.) So, I just picked up a message from Emily, she and Susan are going to a poetry reading together!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So! Poetry? Susan's gay! They're being gay together!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Emily's straight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, wake up!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, Carol really messed you up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, she turned you into this-this-this untrusting, crazy, jealous, sycophant. (They all look at her.) All right, so I don't know what sycophant means, but the rest is right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I don't know what you're talking about, I am not a crazy, jealous person.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She's totally right! When we were together, you got all freaked out about Mark and there was nothing going on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This totally makes sense!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It does not!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, sure it does! In high school, you weren't jealous at all even though all your girlfriends were cheating on you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, all right, so up until '92-93 he was very trusting, then '94 hit, Carol left him and bamn! Paranoid city!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Absolutely! Absolutely!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so much fun!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is not fun!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, all we're trying to say is, don't let what happened with Carol ruin what you got with Emily.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. The '92 Ross wouldn't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I still think I was right about that whole Mark thing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What-yeah-what, y'know what? I hope Emily is a lesbian.
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Phoebe is showing off more of her drum skills to Joey by rubbing one of the sticks back and forth across the drum.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Drum roll.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Okay. Okay. All right. Help! Am I a Mark, or a John?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nah, you're not tall enough to be a Mark, but you might make a good Barney.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right look, am I serious, okay? Tomorrow at 3:30 I am going down to the courthouse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're actually going through with this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, look, this name has been holding me back my entire life. Okay, it's probably why kids picked on me in school, and why I never do well with women... So, as of 4 o'clock tomorrow, I'm either gonna be Mark Johnson or John Markson.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You got problems because of you! Not your name! All right, this has got to stop! Chandler is a great name! In fact-yes, (To Joey) I'm, I'm sorry. I know you really wanted me to name the baby Joey, but eh, so, I'm-I'm, I'm gonna, I'm gonna name the baby Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pleased) Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but you have to keep the name too!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You wanna hug it out?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!
They both hug.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay-oh-yay! Okay, I gotta go tell Frank and Alice! Right now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, uh... (She grabs her coat and runs out.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye, Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, bye!
She exits, and after the door is closed, Chandler turns to Joey and...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ha! Ha! Ha!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh! (Realises it was all a trick to get Phoebe to name the baby Chandler.)
Closing Credits
The Airport, Carol and Ross are waiting for Emily and Susan to deplane. A gorgeous woman walks by and they both turn to watch her go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nice luggage.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: I was gonna say...
Susan and Emily get off.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Hey!
They both run and hug they're respective partners.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Hey! I missed you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I missed you too.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: (To Emily) Thanks for everything, I had such a great time.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh, so did I.
They hug and give each other a little peck on the cheek.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Carol) No tongue. (And gives her the thumbs up.)
End
